title: DIY Parsons Small Coffee Table
summary: A small square Parsons-style coffee table — flush 2x2 legs, a joined 1x12 panel top, and independently built side frames that hide every pocket hole.
description:
The Parsons silhouette is defined by legs that run flush with the top's edges rather than being inset under an apron — clean square corners, no visible framing. Here that's achieved by building the top panel first from joined 1x12 boards, then attaching 2x2 legs directly beneath it, and finally building independent side frames that wrap the assembly and hide every pocket hole on their underside.

The side frames are the one part worth building off the table rather than in place: assemble each one flat on the bench, square, glued, with pocket holes facing down, then attach the finished frames to the shelf assembly. That sequence keeps the visible faces clean and the joinery invisible — the whole Parsons look depends on not seeing how it's held together.

cutList.note: Measure and cut to fit your actual glued-up panel width.

steps.title: Join the top panel
steps.body: Attach the four 1x12 boards edge to edge to form the shelf/top panel.

steps.title: Attach the under-top supports
steps.body: Drill a 1-1/2" pocket hole on each end of the 2x2 supports. Attach them to the underside of the panel with 1-1/4" pocket hole screws.

steps.title: Build and attach the leg frames
steps.body:
Build the leg side frames independently, joined with 1-1/2" pocket hole screws and glue — one pocket hole per joint is enough if you're also gluing. Keep every pocket hole on the underside where it won't show.

Attach the assembled leg frames to the top panel: 1-1/4" pocket hole screws for the 3/4" holes, 2-1/2" pocket hole screws for the 1-1/2" holes.

steps.title: Sand and finish
steps.body: Sand all surfaces smooth, then finish.
